judgment.summary: Visa demonstrates robust profitability, strong growth, and excellent returns, trading at a premium valuation supported by high analyst conviction.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 6.8%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Profit margin 51.7% — exceptionally high and indicative of strong pricing power.
Return on equity 60.3% — outstanding profitability relative to shareholder equity.
Revenue growth 17.1% YoY and Earnings growth 35.5% YoY — demonstrating significant top and bottom-line expansion.
Free cash flow $20.84B TTM — substantial cash generation supports operations and shareholder returns.
Consensus 'strong_buy' from 35 analysts with a mean target of $398.74.
negatives: Forward P/E 22.31x and PEG ratio 1.73x — valuation is elevated, pricing in future growth.
Total debt $23.98B vs $13.91B cash — leverage is present, though manageable given cash flow.
Current ratio 1.09 and Quick ratio 0.67 — liquidity is adequate but not abundant.

news.paragraph: Visa (V) is being highlighted by Wall Street analysts as a potentially profitable stock to buy, according to recent commentary. While no specific catalysts are mentioned, the ongoing positive analyst sentiment and strong consensus ratings suggest a favorable outlook. Traders should consider the current price relative to analyst targets and the upcoming earnings date.

headlines.title: Is Visa (V) Best Extremely Profitable Stocks To Buy According To Wall Street Analysts?
